## Codex runtime adapter

- Use Codex collaboration tools (`spawn_agent`, `send_message`, `wait_agent`, `list_agents`, `interrupt_agent`) when they are available.
- Codex has no Claude Agent Team shared runtime. The parent agent owns wave state, checkpoints, aggregation, and conflict resolution.
- Keep `.claude/**` as the editable source of truth. Read generated `.agents/**` and `.codex/**` for runtime discovery, but never edit it directly.
- After changing `CLAUDE.md` or `.claude/**`, run `python .claude/shared/scripts/sync_to_codex.py --project . --apply --prune`.


# Command workflow: /end-session

Treat Claude-only command syntax as workflow intent and use the available Codex tools.

End of Agent Team working session.

Steps:
1. Check the status of active tasks — identify any incomplete ones
2. Launch the meta-reviewer:
   spawn_agent(task_name="meta-reviewer", message="Analyze the session...")
3. Wait for the report
4. Append lessons to .claude/agent-memory/team-lead/MEMORY.md under a new session entry
5. Output a summary to the user:
   - Tasks completed: N of M
   - Key outcomes
   - What remains for the next session
   - Which improvements were applied

If there are incomplete tasks — save them to work/backlog.md.
